name: Black Stones Route – O Grove Peninsula
name_original: Ruta das Pedras Negras — Península de O Grove
description:
The diversity of Galicia makes it famous for many facets. O Grove, in particular, attracts many people for its fine sand beaches, but here is also hidden one of the most beautiful trails in the Galician community.

The Pedras Negras Trail is perfect if what you are looking for is to walk by the sea and learn some information about the history of the place. The first part of the itinerary runs along wooden walkways and, later, moves on to a dirt path that takes you to the same point where you started. Along the way, you will see stones of all shapes and sizes. 
In the end, you arrive at the O Grove military battery, which hides a great anecdote: in the 1950s, in the midst of the Cold War, the Americans described it as one of the most efficient defense systems that existed.

We recommend the hottest months to venture to O Grove. This way, you will avoid the rain and you can take a swim in the Atlantic. If you need to use public transport, there are buses from Vigo and Pontevedra.
region_intro: Hiking around O Grove offers diverse coastal landscapes and natural features on a peninsula almost entirely surrounded by the sea. The region is characterized by picturesque beaches, unique rock formations, and protected natural areas like the Intertidal Complex Umia-O Grove-A Lanzada. Trails often provide views of the Atlantic Ocean and nearby islands, including La Toja. The terrain typically features gentle elevation changes along the coastline. Best hiking trails around O Grove The most popular hiking route is Black Stones Route – O Grove Peninsula, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic coastline, offering views of coves and beaches. Another top favourite among local hikers is O Grove Coastal Loop, a difficult 12.2 miles (19.6 km) path. This challenging loop provides continuous coastal views and opportunities to observe unique rock formations. Local hikers also love the La Toja Island Loop, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through the island's natural surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Hiking around O Grove is defined by extensive coastal paths, diverse beaches, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging loops. The routes in O Grove are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 140 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore O Grove's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in O Grove?
A: O Grove offers a diverse coastal landscape, almost entirely surrounded by the sea. You'll encounter picturesque beaches, unique rock formations, and protected natural areas like the Intertidal Complex Umia-O Grove-A Lanzada. Many trails provide breathtaking views of the Atlantic Ocean and nearby islands, including La Toja.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in O Grove?
A: Yes, O Grove has many easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991706843/la-toja-island-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Toja Island Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an accessible 2.8-mile (4.6 km) path that takes about 1 hour 12 minutes to complete, leading through the island's natural surroundings. There are also several easy sections of the coastal boardwalks.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in O Grove?
A: O Grove is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. The region's mild climate makes it suitable for outdoor activities, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, especially for combining hikes with beach visits, though it can be warmer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in O Grove?
A: Many of O Grove's coastal paths and boardwalks are suitable for families, offering gentle terrain and scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991706843/la-toja-island-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Toja Island Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for families due to its accessibility and relatively short distance. Look for trails with minimal elevation changes for the most comfortable family experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in O Grove?
A: Many trails and coastal promenades in O Grove are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them. Specific rules may vary by location, so observe local signage.

Q: What kind of unique natural features or landmarks can I discover along the trails?
A: O Grove's trails are rich in natural beauty. You can find unique rock formations, especially along the coast, and explore the protected Intertidal Complex Umia-O Grove-A Lanzada. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4653902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Carreiro Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt; are a notable highlight. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3494256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Grove Coastal Military Battery&lt;/a&gt;, offering a historical perspective amidst the natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in O Grove?
A: Yes, O Grove offers several circular routes that allow you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991712719/o-grove-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Grove Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 12.2-mile (19.6 km) option with continuous coastal views. For an easier circular walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Stones Coastal Boardwalk – Ruta das Pedras Negras loop from Praia de Con Negro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical elevation change on hiking trails in O Grove?
A: The terrain in O Grove typically features gentle elevation changes, especially along the coastline. Most routes are relatively flat, making them accessible. Even longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497797911/black-stones-route-o-grove-peninsula" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Stones Route – O Grove Peninsula&lt;/a&gt; have modest elevation gains, usually under 100 meters, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991712719/o-grove-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Grove Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; has about 170 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the hiking trails in O Grove?
A: Absolutely. O Grove is known for its stunning coastal vistas. Many trails offer panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean, the surrounding cliffs, and nearby islands. Miradoiro da Siradella is a well-known viewpoint offering extensive vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991712719/o-grove-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Grove Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides continuous scenic outlooks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in O Grove?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 140 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal scenery, the diversity of beaches, the unique rock formations, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to various abilities.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, O Grove is a popular tourist destination, and you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and pubs, especially in the main town and along the coastal areas. Many establishments are conveniently located near trailheads or popular sections of the coastal boardwalks, offering opportunities to refuel after your hike.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in O Grove?
A: Public transport options exist within O Grove and connect it to nearby towns. While specific bus routes to every trailhead might vary, the main town and popular areas like La Toja Island are well-served. For more remote starting points, checking local bus schedules or considering a taxi might be necessary.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near the hiking trails?
A: O Grove boasts a long coastline with numerous beautiful beaches, many of which are perfect for swimming. Praia da Lanzada is renowned for its clear waters, and other significant beaches like Raeiros and Area da Cruz are easily accessible from coastal hiking paths, offering opportunities for a refreshing dip after your walk.
tips: This lonely and wild beach does not usually attract many crowds in summer. Perfect for those looking for peace and tranquility.
This military battery has such a strategic location that in the 1950s, in the midst of the Cold War, the United States recognized that it was one of the most effective defense systems in the world.
The rocks along the trail are completely spectacular.
